detector: tripwire-core (same build as the internal eval); direction=Inbound, source=User

datasets.name: deepset
datasets.title: deepset/prompt-injections
datasets.cite: Canonical academic binary injection set (EN + DE). ~38% of the injection rows are German; the injection class is dominated by roleplay / persona / context-switch prompts.

datasets.name: gandalf
datasets.title: Lakera/gandalf_ignore_instructions
datasets.cite: Real prompts that successfully extracted the password from Lakera's Gandalf game. All-attack, so this measures recall only -- a mix of instruction-override and pure social-engineering extraction.

datasets.name: safeguard
datasets.title: xTRam1/safe-guard-prompt-injection (test split)
datasets.cite: Held-out test split of a larger binary injection set. A second, independent, larger precision/recall probe.

datasets.name: notinject
datasets.title: leolee99/NotInject (over-defense set)
datasets.cite: Benign prompts built to contain injection trigger words, designed to measure over-defense (false positives). All-benign, so this stress-tests the near-zero-false-positive claim against adversarial negatives.

unavailable.name: PINT
unavailable.title: Lakera PINT Benchmark
unavailable.reason: The PINT test set (4,314 inputs) is intentionally NOT publicly released -- Lakera keeps it private so detectors cannot train on it. The github.com/lakeraai/pint-benchmark repo ships the harness but not the data (request-gated). We do not fabricate a PINT score. For context only, Lakera's published leaderboard reports deepset's DeBERTa-v3 injection model around 62% and Lakera Guard around 92.5% on PINT.
